column1: # ZAP Baseline rules file - https://www.zaproxy.org/docs/docker/baseline-scan/
# Format: <rule id>
# Genuine, actionable findings (CSP, Permissions-Policy, cookie SameSite,
# see 10038 / 10063 / 10054) are FIXED IN CODE (SecurityConfig.java,
# application.yml) rather than silenced here - only findings reviewed and
# judged to be false positives or accepted low-priority risk for the scope
# of this assignment are downgraded below. See docs/dast-findings.md for
# the full write-up required by the assignment brief.
# Informational scan-metadata alerts, not vulnerabilities: ZAP confirming
# it recognised a login form / session response. Nothing to fix.

column4: Authentication Request Identified - informational confirmation that ZAP found the login form.
Session Management Response Identified - informational confirmation, not a finding.

column1: # Reviewed and judged false positive: our Thymeleaf templates use th:field,
# which HTML-escapes all output by default; no th:utext (raw/unescaped
# output) is used anywhere in the codebase, so reflected user input cannot
# break out of the attribute context.

column4: User Controllable HTML Element Attribute (Potential XSS) - Thymeleaf auto-escapes th:field output; no unescaped th:utext used.

column1: # Reviewed and judged false positive: no debug/TODO/FIXME comments exist in
# our HTML templates; matches on generic HTML boilerplate.

column4: Information Disclosure - Suspicious Comments - no debug comments present in templates (verified manually).

column1: # Reviewed and judged false positive: the pattern match is on the CSRF
# token / session identifier, which are expected opaque tokens, not
# secrets that are meaningful outside an authenticated session.

column4: Base64 Disclosure - matches expected CSRF/session tokens, not a leaked secret.

column1: # Accepted low priority for this assignment's scope: these headers matter
# mainly for cross-origin resource isolation (SharedArrayBuffer, COOP/COEP)
# and browser-side fetch metadata, neither of which this simple
# server-rendered app relies on.

column4: Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy Header Missing - no cross-origin isolation requirement (no SharedArrayBuffer/worker usage).
Sec-Fetch-Dest Header is Missing - this is a request header sent by the browser, not one the server can set.

column1: # Accepted low priority: response caching headers on public, non-sensitive
# pages (login/register) and on 401 responses for robots.txt/sitemap.xml,
# which do not exist and carry no sensitive data.

column4: Non-Storable Content - informational, no sensitive data is served on these routes.
